channel.title: # 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 310 English News Articles published between 2026-06-12 and 2026-06-19.

item.title: A US–Iran memorandum paused hostilities and signalled the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description: Benchmark crude slid to below $80 a barrel, a three‑month low, trimming a surcharge that had raised import costs and pressured prices. Equities in Colombo advanced as risk premia eased, with turnover supported by crossings and broad gains across banking and capital‑goods counters. The exchange rate steadied after weeks of volatility, while bond yields eased along the curve. Officials said shipping lanes would clear gradually as mine‑removal and insurance reinstatement progressed (the process by which underwriters resume cover after hostilities) over coming weeks.

item.title: The economy grew 5.1% in the first quarter of 2026.
item.description: Output at constant prices reached Rs. 3.65 trillion, up from Rs. 3.48 trillion a year earlier. Industry expanded 7.2%, led by a 16.3% surge in construction and a 19.5% rise in mining and quarrying. Services rose 3.4% with double‑digit gains in insurance and IT, while agriculture edged up 1.1% as tea and rubber weakened. At current prices, gross domestic product increased 11% to Rs. 9.16 trillion (the money value of all final goods and services in an economy).

item.title: Sri Lanka launched a five‑year export plan targeting US$ 36 billion by 2030.
item.description: The roadmap set priorities across eight sectors, from digital services and electronics to boat building and processed foods. Cross‑cutting actions included trade facilitation, standards, skills and logistics, developed with technical support from the Asian Development Bank (a multilateral development bank that lends and advises in Asia). Officials said the aim was to broaden the basket and deepen value‑chain participation, moving beyond promotion to tackle bottlenecks. The programme aligns with fiscal reforms and seeks non‑debt foreign exchange to strengthen reserve buffers.

item.title: A nationwide dengue drive intensified as admissions strained hospitals.
item.description: Authorities focused inspections on schools, factories, offices and homes during a designated control week. Health leaders warned of overcrowding and reported breeding sites at 41% of inspected schools, 28% of religious places and 26% of industrial locations. Campaigns prioritised source reduction, with clean‑up operations, enforcement and public messaging. Planners cautioned that timely destruction of breeding grounds is the most effective short‑term relief for case loads.

item.title: A senior officials’ committee was appointed to study a unified, tripartite governance model for EPF and ETF.
item.description: The review will assess feasibility of integrating oversight with a board representing government, employers and employees (a tripartite structure that shares decision‑making among three constituencies). Combined member assets exceed Rs. 5.5 trillion across the two funds. Analysts said aligning with International Labour Organisation principles could strengthen safeguards and transparency. Any changes would require legislative amendments and operational transition plans.

item.title: A court ordered the arrest of a former minister in an alleged 2014 public‑funds case.
item.description: Magistrates directed production before court in a matter concerning Rs. 7.8 million reportedly spent on T‑shirts during a provincial poll. Investigators said charges were filed under the Public Property Act (a statute that criminalises misappropriation of state assets). Another suspect received bail with a travel ban, while further action awaits guidance from the Attorney General.

item.title: A bank said its takeover of a multinational’s retail and wealth business would complete by 31 July.
item.description: The acquirer disclosed the timeline in a filing after obtaining prior approvals. The transaction follows a regional exit by the seller and is expected to bolster affluent and consumer segments. Market watchers said integration would add scale ahead of a gradual credit upturn. A separate lender opened a Rs. 10–15 billion debenture issue to strengthen Tier 2 capital buffers.

item.title: Tea production in May fell 2.1% year‑on‑year to 24.91 million kilogrammes.
item.description: High‑ and medium‑grown output contracted, while low‑grown volumes rose slightly to 14.51 million kilogrammes. Cumulative January–May production declined 4.5% to 108.95 million kilogrammes compared with a year earlier. Compared with 2024, the five‑month total was higher by 4.15 million kilogrammes as weather and field conditions improved from a lower base.

item.title: The airport launched a Rs. 15 billion project to upgrade an ageing terminal.
item.description: Plans include expanded departures, redesigned arrivals and modernised baggage systems to ease congestion. Officials said overall capacity would rise to about 15 million passengers annually once works finish. The interim upgrade bridges demand until a Japan‑assisted new terminal is delivered. Funding was arranged domestically to accelerate timelines.

item.title: Partial services on the upcountry railway were set to resume after cyclone damage repairs.
item.description: Engineers completed works between key hill‑country stations, enabling operations from the weekend under a special timetable. Additional stretches remain under rehabilitation after landslides and washouts. Authorities said further sections would reopen in phases as stability testing concludes. Freight and long‑distance links are being prioritised alongside commuter services.

item.title: The road‑safety regulator fixed a date to strictly enforce seat belts on expressways for all occupants.
item.description: Officials ended the grace period and confirmed fines would apply from 20 June. Rear‑seat compliance is mandatory on high‑speed corridors, while front‑seat rules continue on other roads. Police cited recent collisions where belted occupants suffered fewer injuries. Campaigns are targeting retrofitting and public awareness to lift usage rates.
